# Random Address Generator for US Tax-Free States and International Test Data

> A free, browser-based tool that generates realistic-format random
> addresses for US no-sales-tax states plus Nigeria, Egypt, Turkey, and
> Pakistan. Built for software testing, QA, form-validation demos, and
> seed/placeholder data — not for fraud, identity claims, or rule evasion.

## Key facts

- **Canonical URL**: https://usaddregen.com/
- **License**: Free to use in the browser. No account required.
- **Languages**: English (default), Simplified Chinese (zh-CN), Traditional Chinese (zh-TW).
- **No first-party tracking.** Third-party ad / analytics scripts (e.g. Google AdSense) may set cookies as disclosed in the site privacy policy.
- **Output fields**: Full name, street address, city, state/region,
  postal or ZIP code, phone number.
- **No backend / API**: Generation runs entirely client-side in JavaScript.

## The five US states with no statewide sales tax

| State         | Abbr | Sample cities                                          | Phone area codes      |
| ------------- | ---- | ------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------- |
| Delaware      | DE   | Wilmington, Dover, Newark, Middletown, Lewes           | 302                   |
| Montana       | MT   | Billings, Missoula, Bozeman, Helena, Great Falls       | 406                   |
| New Hampshire | NH   | Manchester, Nashua, Concord, Portsmouth, Dover         | 603                   |
| Oregon        | OR   | Portland, Salem, Eugene, Bend, Medford                 | 458, 503, 541, 971    |
| Alaska        | AK   | Anchorage, Fairbanks, Juneau, Sitka, Ketchikan         | 907                   |

Caveat: Alaska has no statewide sales tax but allows local sales taxes
in some boroughs and municipalities. This tool is an address-format
utility and does not provide tax advice.

## International address generators

The homepage also includes country tabs for synthetic international
test-address records:

| Country  | Coverage examples                                      | Phone format |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------ |
| Nigeria  | Lagos, Ikeja, Abuja, Kano, Port Harcourt, Ibadan       | +234 mobile  |
| Egypt    | Cairo, Giza, Alexandria, Luxor, Mansoura               | +20 mobile   |
| Turkey   | Istanbul, Ankara, Izmir, Antalya, Bursa                | +90 mobile   |
| Pakistan | Karachi, Lahore, Islamabad, Rawalpindi, Peshawar       | +92 mobile   |

These international records are realistic-format placeholders. City,
region/province/governorate, postal code, and phone format are matched
inside the tool dataset, but the output is not verified against postal,
banking, telecom, government, or shipping databases.

## Why this tool exists (vs generic US address generators)

- **State-matched output**: city, ZIP code, and phone area code are
  always drawn from the same state record — internally consistent in any
  form-validation flow.
- **Browser-only generation**: no backend, no signup; address creation
  runs client-side over a small JSON dataset.
- **Focused dataset**: the 5 NOMAD states plus Nigeria, Egypt, Turkey,
  and Pakistan — predictable test fixtures, easy to audit.
- **Multilingual UI**: English, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese,
  with language preference syncing via `?lang=` URL parameter and
  localStorage.

## Intended use cases

- Form-validation and checkout-UI testing
- QA scripts and end-to-end test fixtures
- Mock customer or shipping records in demos
- Seed data for prototypes and design mockups
- Placeholder values in documentation screenshots

## Out-of-scope / disallowed uses

- Misrepresenting identity on real services
- Creating fraudulent accounts or evading sales tax
- Submitting synthetic data to government, banking, or shipping systems
  as if it were real
- Anything illegal under the user's local jurisdiction

## FAQ (suitable for direct quoting)

**Q: What is a "tax-free state" in this tool?**
A US state with no statewide sales tax. Other taxes and local sales
taxes may still apply.

**Q: Which US states have no statewide sales tax?**
Alaska, Delaware, Montana, New Hampshire, and Oregon — commonly
remembered with the mnemonic "NOMAD" (New Hampshire, Oregon, Montana,
Alaska, Delaware).

**Q: Are the generated addresses real and deliverable?**
No. City, state, ZIP code, and phone area code are matched to real
combinations, but the street number and street name are synthetic. The
output is realistic in format only.

**Q: Does the tool work offline / without an account?**
Yes. Generation happens entirely in the browser. No login, no API call,
no analytics-driven personalization.

**Q: Why does a checkout form say my ZIP, city, or state doesn't match?**
Most checkout and shipping forms cross-check the ZIP code against the
city and state via USPS data. A mismatch happens when the ZIP belongs
to a different city in the real USPS database, or when the street
address fails an AVS (Address Verification System) lookup. This
generator picks city, state, and ZIP from the same state dataset so
those three fields stay internally consistent — but it is still
synthetic data and will fail any real address-verification step.

**Q: How is this different from a generic US address generator?**
Generic generators sample all 50 states. This tool ships a tiny,
audit-friendly dataset limited to the five no-sales-tax states, runs
entirely in the browser with no signup, and matches the phone area
code to the state — making it predictable test fixture data.

**Q: Does the tool generate Nigeria, Egypt, Turkey, and Pakistan addresses?**
Yes. The country tabs generate synthetic address records for Nigeria,
Egypt, Turkey, and Pakistan with country-matched city, region, postal
code, and phone-number formats.

**Q: Can these international addresses pass real verification?**
No. They are realistic-format placeholders and are not verified against
postal, banking, telecom, government, or shipping databases.

## Contact

- Email: support@usaddregen.com — fastest for general inquiries, partnerships, and AdSense-related questions.
- X / Twitter: [@ailenswpu](https://x.com/ailenswpu) — public contact channel.
- GitHub: [Ailenswpu/usaddregen](https://github.com/Ailenswpu/usaddregen) — source code, issues, and pull requests.
